title: Functions as Values, Signatures, and Composition · Side-Effect Repair Bay
summary: Turn repeated validation branches into reusable transformations for an intake service. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a typed composition library with signature evidence and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pass, store, return, and inspect callables while preserving clear input-output contracts.
Model callable parameter and return relationships with precise type annotations.
Compose compatible unary transformations and explain data flow across boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Composition Law Tests value-and-effect pipeline for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Composition Law Tests assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Three intake records need normalize → validate → score transformations. A rejected record must retain its reason, and adding a fourth transformation must not change earlier function contracts.

content.text: Order work so Composition Law Tests has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
